mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant or forward returns remain positive when both series accelerate.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in industrial production index values reflect robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive short-term equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside late-cycle expansion regimes or produces negative returns during supply surges.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained economic momentum and consumer spending capacity, which supports higher corporate revenues and yields positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or payroll gains fail to predict positive 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below their 12-month moving average indicates a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when housing starts drop below the 12-month trend.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising consumer price inflation and increasing federal funds rates creates a hostile financial environment that raises borrowing costs and erodes real cash flows, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is statistically insignificant or forward returns are positive when both inflation and rates rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly real GDP growth signals broad-based economic expansion and robust corporate profit growth, which drives positive equity returns over the next quarter during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during post-shock recovery regimes or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.
